mechanism_narrative: A high level of housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust construction activity and consumer confidence, which supports economic growth and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If over a 2-year rolling window, instances with HOUST above 1500 produce forward returns not significantly greater than zero (t-test p>0.10) or rank IC<0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) falls below 5%, it signals a tight labor market that may lead to wage inflation and margin compression, reducing corporate earnings and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If in triggered instances (UNRATE<5%), the 60-day forward return is positive more than 50% of the time, or the mean return is not significantly negative at 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: When the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 200, it indicates elevated inflation that prompts the Federal Reserve to adopt a tightening stance, increasing discount rates and depressing equity valuations over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: If over any 3-year period, the signal (CPIAUCSL>200) is followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 40% of instances, or the average return is not statistically negative.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 5%) and declining housing starts (HOUST < 2000) signals a coordinated credit tightening and weakening housing demand, which contractionary pressure reduces corporate profits and equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint signal fails to predict negative returns in at least 60% of triggered instances over a 5-year walk-forward period, or if the interaction term in a linear regression is not statistically significant at 5%.

mechanism_narrative: In the current regime of elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL>200), a decline in unemployment signifies labor market tightness that exacerbates wage pressures, forcing the Fed to tighten policy and thereby weighing on stock prices; in a low-inflation regime the same decline would be positive for stocks.
failure_condition: If the conditional negative relationship fails to hold in out-of-sample testing: i.e., the average 20-day return in triggered instances during high-inflation periods is not negative or is not significantly different from returns during low-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: When both payroll employment (PAYEMS) is above 135 million and retail sales (RSAFS) exceed $360 billion, it signals synchronized strength in labor income and consumption, which drives corporate earnings higher and boosts stock prices over the next month.
failure_condition: If the joint signal produces positive returns less than 60% of the time over a 3-year window, or if the interaction effect is not positive when tested via a multifactor model.

mechanism_narrative: A high level of real GDP (GDPC1 above 11000) indicates sustained aggregate demand and economic output, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between GDPC1 level and 60-day forward returns is non-positive over any 10-year window, or if the mean return following GDPC1>11000 is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) is above 110 and retail sales (RSAFS) are above $360 billion, the economy exhibits both strong supply capacity and consumer demand, a healthy economic mix that boosts corporate profits and stock prices within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the signal's information coefficient (IC) falls below 0.04 over a 3-year rolling window, or if a multivariate regression shows no significant positive interaction coefficient.
